title: chrysopsis gossypina var. dentata
intro: Chrysopsis gossypina var. dentata, also known as the hairy goldenaster, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chrysopsis gossypina var. dentata include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its leaves and flowers, or by infusing its dried flowers into oil for topical application.
